task:
OWNER MAP ONLY — produce the pre-implementation owner map required by this priority's Boundary (ADR-0010 / durable-orchestration rule). DO NOT change any product behavior, prompts, or runtime code in this atom; this is a read-and-document atom. Write the result to `docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md` (create it).

The priority is 'Oz hardening — first-class chat, self-refreshing run-state awareness, drag-to-ask context'. Read the actual code; do not guess. The owner map must contain these sections, and every named file MUST be a real path you verified exists (cite path:line where you can):

1. RUN-STATE PROJECTION SOURCE OF TRUTH — Identify the single durable source of active-run/session and ticket state that Oz's awareness should project from (NOT chat scrollback). Where does active run/session state live on disk / in the daemon? Where do committed tickets under `cocoder/tickets/open/` get read? Name the one owner that items 2 (refresh/compact read path) and item 4 (change-detection path) must SHARE.

2. OZ MESSAGE & STATUS RENDER SURFACES — Enumerate every surface that renders Oz messages or status: the daemon Oz loop (where Oz produces responses / where reasoning vs final tokens are available, if at all), `OzChat.tsx` (the terminal/chat renderer), and the status feed/projection. For each: file path, what it currently owns, and how messages currently flow (plain text? markdown? streaming or single-shot?).

3. STREAMING & THINKING CAPABILITY — Determine factually whether the Oz runtime currently exposes (a) incremental/streaming tokens and (b) separate reasoning/thinking tokens. Cite where in the code the Oz response is produced/consumed. This decides whether item 1's streaming + show-thinking-if-available is wiring-up vs net-new plumbing.

4. SETTINGS SURFACE — Locate the settings owner where a new field 'Oz Auto Compact at N Runs' (default 3, range 2-10) would live, so it is one owner not a parallel toggle. Name the file(s) for settings schema, persistence, and UI.

5. DRAG-TO-ASK — Identify how dashboard work items (priority/run/ticket) are represented in the dashboard UI and what drop-target plumbing (if any) exists on the Oz terminal, so a lightweight pointer (file path + slug) can be injected.

6. TESTS/FIXTURES THAT PIN THESE — List the existing tests/fixtures covering the Oz loop, OzChat rendering, status projection, settings, and ticket/run pickup. These are what must be updated/added.

7. COORDINATION & ADJACENT GAPS — Note the seam with archived `workspace-segmentation` (it owned Oz terminal PANEL LAYOUT/global controls; THIS priority owns chat RENDERING QUALITY inside that panel) so implementation won't clobber it. Review tickets 0013 (daemon auto-rebuild after runs) and 0015 (tickets silently dropped without frontmatter) and the run_131 symptom (committed ticket 0014 not appearing in Oz); state whether item 4 subsumes each.

8. PROPOSED ATOM SEQUENCE — End with a recommended ordered decomposition into implementable atoms (decisions/taxonomy -> shared projection engine (items 2&4) -> render quality (item 1) -> drag-to-ask (item 3) -> proof), each naming its exit criterion and whether it is loop-amenable.

ACCEPTANCE: `docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md` exists with all 8 sections; every file path named resolves to a real file; the shared projection owner for items 2&4 is named explicitly; and the streaming/thinking capability question is answered with a code citation, not a guess. Touch ONLY `docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md`.

ITEM 1 (part A) — RICH MARKDOWN RENDERING for Oz chat messages in `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/OzChat.tsx`. This is the render-quality atom from the owner map's atom sequence (step 3). Do NOT touch streaming or thinking-token plumbing in this atom — those are a separate later atom. Do NOT rework the Dashboard grid, workspace tabs, ResizeHandle, panel-ratio, or global controls (owned by the archived workspace-segmentation seam). WHAT TO CHANGE: Today `ChatMessageView` (OzChat.tsx:9-49) renders the message body with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` applying only a single bold regex (OzChat.tsx:20). Replace that with safe, rich markdown rendering for Oz message bodies supporting: headings, unordered and ordered lists, fenced code blocks (rendered in monospace `
` preserving whitespace), inline code, links, and bold/italic.

SAFETY (non-negotiable — this is also an XSS fix): the message body is untrusted runtime text. Do NOT inject unsanitized HTML. Either (a) use markdown via an existing/vetted lightweight dependency that escapes/sanitizes HTML, or (b) render to React elements (not raw HTML strings). First CHECK existing dependencies (`packages/ui/package.json` and the root lockfile) and PREFER a markdown/sanitizer lib already present; only add a new dependency if none exists, and if so pick a well-maintained, lightweight, sanitizing one (e.g. a markdown parser that does not pass through raw HTML, or markdown + a sanitizer). Any raw HTML or `

FIX PRE-EXISTING TYPECHECK REGRESSION in `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/Settings.tsx` (introduced by run_156's addition of the flat `ozAutoCompactRuns` field; NOT a feature change). The package typecheck is currently RED: `tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json` reports `src/renderer/sections/Settings.tsx(88,42): error TS2698: Spread types may only be created from object types`.

ROOT CAUSE (verify, don't take on faith): the renderer `Settings` model (in `packages/ui/src/renderer/model.ts`) is a mix of SECTION OBJECTS (`preferences`, `watching`, `advanced`) and now a TOP-LEVEL SCALAR `ozAutoCompactRuns: number` (added run_156). The generic helper `const update = <S extends keyof Settings>(section: S, key: keyof Settings[S], value) => onChange({ ...settings, [section]: { ...settings[section], [key]: value } })` (Settings.tsx:87-88) spreads `settings[section]`, but `keyof Settings` now includes the scalar key `ozAutoCompactRuns` whose value (`number`) is not spreadable. `update` is only ever called with section keys ('preferences' | 'watching' | 'advanced'); the flat scalar uses the separate `updateSetting` helper (Settings.tsx:89).

REQUIRED FIX (root cause, not a cast/`any` escape hatch): narrow `update`'s generic so its `section` parameter only accepts keys of `Settings` whose value is an object (the section keys) — e.g. introduce a `SettingsSectionKey` type like `{ [K in keyof Settings]: Settings[K] extends object ? K : never }[keyof Settings]` and use `<S extends SettingsSectionKey>`. This makes `settings[section]` provably spreadable and preserves the existing per-section update behavior. Do NOT change runtime behavior, do NOT restructure the Settings model, do NOT touch the daemon settings type or the settings-sync path, and do NOT silence the error with `as any`/`@ts-ignore`.

DEFECT-CLASS CHECK: confirm there is no symmetric breakage — scan Settings.tsx (and model.ts if you add the helper type there) for any other spot that spreads or indexes a top-level scalar `Settings` key as if it were a section. Report what you found.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE: `pnpm --dir packages/ui run typecheck` exits 0 (BOTH tsconfig.json and tsconfig.app.json projects clean), and `pnpm --dir packages/ui test` still passes all suites. Provide the exact commands and exit status. Write scope: `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/Settings.tsx` and, only if you place the section-key type there, `packages/ui/src/renderer/model.ts`. Touch nothing else.

DESIGN + CAPABILITY PROBE for Oz response STREAMING and SHOW-THINKING-IF-AVAILABLE (item 1, part B). This is a READ-ONLY design atom — do NOT change any product/daemon/UI/adapter code. Output one artifact: `docs/oz-streaming-design.md`. The goal is an evidence-backed go/no-go and, if go, an ordered build plan; if no-go, the exact founder decision. Order: decisions before schema before build.

CONTEXT (verify against code, don't take on faith): Oz runs headless via the codex adapter as `codex exec --output-last-message <outPath>` (`packages/adapters/src/codex.ts:34-41`); `runTurn` waits for the process to COMPLETE and reads the whole final message file (`packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts:165-186`). Delivery to the UI is a single-JSON `POST /oz/messages` (`packages/daemon/src/routes.ts`). There is an existing SSE channel `/oz/events` for refetch hints (`streamOzEvents()` in routes.ts, `OzEventBus`/`emitOzEvent()` in `packages/daemon/src/context.ts`). The renderer awaits one `chatSend()` result (`packages/ui/src/renderer/live.ts`) and the IPC contract carries a single `OzChatReply` with no token chunks (`packages/ui/src/main/ipc-contract.ts`). Markdown rendering already landed (`OzChat.tsx` MarkdownBody).

REQUIRED CONTENT of `docs/oz-streaming-design.md`:
1. CODEX STREAMING CAPABILITY — Probe the installed codex CLI for an incremental/streaming output mode and a separated reasoning/thinking stream. Run real commands and quote the evidence: e.g. `codex exec --help`, `codex --version`, and any `--json`/`--experimental-json`/event-stream/`--include-reasoning`-style flags. State definitively: (a) can `codex exec` emit incremental output before completion? (b) does it expose reasoning/thinking tokens distinctly from the final answer? Quote the actual help/output — do not guess.
2. TRADEOFF vs ARTIFACT-COMPLETION — The current design deliberately reads the clean `--output-last-message` file because raw stdout includes transcript + token footer noise (see codex.ts comment). If streaming requires parsing raw stdout/JSON events instead, state the reliability tradeoff and whether the clean final-answer artifact can still be the source of truth for the COMMITTED transcript while only the in-progress view streams. This bears on the ADR-0006 artifact-completion posture — call that out explicitly.
3. DELIVERY DESIGN (reuse owners, NO parallel contract) — If streaming is feasible, design the incremental delivery path reusing the EXISTING owners: how token/chunk events flow daemon -> main IPC -> renderer model -> `OzChatPanel`. Prefer extending the existing `/oz/events` SSE + `OzEventBus` and the existing IPC `OzChatReply`/event contract, keyed by a turn/message id, rather than inventing a new streaming endpoint or a parallel message contract. Name every file that must change and what field/event each gains. Thinking must be modeled as OPTIONAL (show-thinking-if-available; gracefully absent).
4. GO / NO-GO —
   - If codex CAN stream: give the ORDERED sub-atom sequence for the build (adapter streaming capability/contract change -> daemon incremental delivery over the event owner -> IPC+model schema -> OzChatPanel incremental append + collapsible thinking affordance -> tests), each with its exit criterion. Note which sub-atoms are loop-amenable.
   - If codex CANNOT stream incrementally without unacceptable tradeoffs: say so plainly and state the exact FOUNDER DECISION (e.g. switch Oz off clean artifact-completion onto stdout/JSON-event parsing and accept the noise/reliability tradeoff, vs. keep artifact-completion and drop/defer the streaming requirement, vs. a middle option). Do NOT propose faking streaming by artificially chunking an already-complete reply — name that as a non-option.
5. SCOPE NOTE — confirm this does not disturb the archived workspace-segmentation panel-layout seam.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE: `docs/oz-streaming-design.md` exists; the codex capability question is answered with quoted command output (not a guess); the delivery design names real files and reuses existing event/IPC owners; and there is a clear go/no-go with either an ordered build plan or a single named founder decision. Touch ONLY `docs/oz-streaming-design.md` — no product code.

STREAMING SUB-ATOM 1 — CAPTURE THE CODEX JSONL EVENT-SCHEMA FIXTURE (foundation for the answer-streaming build approved in docs/oz-streaming-design.md). This atom does NOT add the parser or any runtime/UI wiring yet — it produces a committed fixture + an observed-schema note that the next atoms consume.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Run ONE bounded, real probe of the installed codex CLI in JSONL mode, capturing BOTH the stdout JSONL event stream AND the clean final-message artifact, using a TEMP dir (e.g. under the system temp, NOT the repo tree) and a TRIVIAL deterministic prompt so output stays tiny. Example shape (adapt to the real flags; codex-cli 0.137.0 supports `--json` = 'Print events to stdout as JSONL' and `-o/--output-last-message <FILE>`):
   `codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --disable apps --json -o /tmp/oz-jsonl-probe/last.txt "Reply with exactly the single word: ping" > /tmp/oz-jsonl-probe/events.jsonl 2>/tmp/oz-jsonl-probe/stderr.log`
   Bound it: small prompt, a wall-clock timeout, run ONCE. Do not loop. Do not run it inside the repo working dir.
2. From the captured `events.jsonl`, identify the event taxonomy: list every distinct JSONL event `type` (or equivalent discriminator field) observed, and pinpoint EXACTLY which event type + field carries INCREMENTAL ANSWER DELTAS (text emitted before completion), which marks COMPLETION/the final message, and which are non-answer noise (transcript, token/footer, reasoning if any, errors). If no reasoning/thinking event type appears, state that explicitly (confirms thinking is gracefully absent on this CLI).
3. PROVE THE RECONCILIATION (the design's exit criterion): show that (a) answer-delta events arrive BEFORE the completion event, and (b) concatenating the answer deltas reconstructs the same final text as the `--output-last-message` artifact (`last.txt`). Quote the supporting lines/byte-or-char comparison.
4. COMMIT A REDACTED FIXTURE for tests: write the representative JSONL (trimmed to a small but COMPLETE sample that includes at least one answer-delta event, the completion event, and one example of each other observed event type) to a fixtures file the parser tests will consume — place it under the adapters package test fixtures (e.g. `packages/adapters/tests/fixtures/codex-jsonl-stream.jsonl`; if that dir does not exist, create it following the repo's existing fixtures convention). Redact any absolute paths, tokens, session ids, or machine-specific values; keep the structural fields intact so the parser can key on them.
5. DOCUMENT THE OBSERVED SCHEMA: append a concise 'Codex JSONL event schema (observed, codex-cli 0.137.0)' section to `docs/oz-streaming-design.md` mapping each event type to its role (answer-delta | completion | other), naming the exact fields the parser must read for answer text, and noting the absence of a reasoning stream. This keeps the streaming-design doc the single owner of the schema knowledge.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE: the fixture file exists and contains at least one answer-delta event + the completion event; the design doc gains the observed-schema section identifying the answer-delta event type/field; and the reconciliation (deltas precede completion AND reconstruct the artifact text) is demonstrated with quoted evidence in your report. If the live codex probe cannot run (auth/network), STOP and report the exact blocker rather than fabricating a fixture. Write scope: `packages/adapters/tests/fixtures/**` (the new fixture) and `docs/oz-streaming-design.md`. Do NOT add parser code, adapter flag changes, or UI wiring in this atom.

STREAMING SUB-ATOM 1 (RE-PROBE — the prior 'ping' probe was too trivial to prove streaming). Definitively determine whether `codex exec --json` (codex-cli 0.137.0) emits INCREMENTAL answer events DURING generation, or just one whole `agent_message` at the end. This is the decisive go/no-go gate for the entire streaming build — do NOT build any parser/runtime/UI in this atom.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Run ONE bounded, real codex probe in a TEMP dir (not the repo tree) with a prompt that produces a LONG answer generated over several seconds — at least ~25 lines / several hundred characters — so incremental emission (if any) is observable. Example prompt: "Count from 1 to 30, printing each number on its own line, then write a 5-sentence paragraph about the ocean." Use the same flags as before plus `--json` and `-o <tmp>/last.txt`. Bound it with a wall-clock timeout; run ONCE; do not loop.
2. CAPTURE PER-EVENT ARRIVAL TIMING: pipe the JSONL stdout through a reader that timestamps each line as it ARRIVES (e.g. `python3 -c 'import sys,time;[sys.stdout.write(f"{time.time():.3f} {l}") for l in sys.stdin]'`), so the evidence shows WHETHER multiple answer events arrive spread over wall-clock time vs. all at the end. Keep both the raw `events.jsonl` and the timestamped log.
3. ENUMERATE THE FULL EVENT TAXONOMY for this long answer: list every distinct event `type` seen and how many of each. CRITICALLY, determine: does the answer text arrive as (a) ONE whole `item.completed`/`agent_message` at the end, or (b) MULTIPLE incremental events over time (look for any `item.delta`, `item.updated`, `item.started`+partial, repeated `agent_message` chunks, or similar)? Quote the timestamped lines that prove which.
4. STATE THE DECISIVE VERDICT in the report and in `docs/oz-streaming-design.md` (correct the schema section — replace the prior 'ping' findings, do not leave both):
   - IF incremental answer events stream over wall-clock time -> STREAMING FEASIBLE. Commit a representative redacted fixture at `packages/adapters/tests/fixtures/codex-jsonl-stream.jsonl` that now contains MULTIPLE real answer-delta events plus the completion event, and confirm the concatenated deltas reconstruct the `--output-last-message` artifact. The design's GO stands.
   - IF the answer arrives as a single whole event at completion (no mid-stream answer deltas) -> TOKEN-LEVEL STREAMING IS NOT AVAILABLE on this codex version. Say so plainly. Note whether even message-level events arrive before full process exit (partial benefit) or not. Update the design doc's Go/No-Go to reflect that the delta assumption is FALSE, and frame the remaining choice as a FOUNDER DECISION (build only message-level/no streaming and defer true streaming, vs. pursue an alternate streaming-capable runtime). Do NOT fabricate delta events and do NOT fake streaming by chunking a completed reply.
5. Either way, the fixture must reflect REALITY (do not keep a fixture that implies deltas if none occur), and the doc must end with an evidence-backed go/no-go.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE: timestamped capture is shown; the verdict (incremental vs whole-message) is stated with quoted timestamped lines as proof; the fixture matches reality; the design doc's schema + go/no-go sections are corrected to the actual capability. If the live probe cannot run (auth/network), STOP and report the exact blocker. Write scope: `packages/adapters/tests/fixtures/**` and `docs/oz-streaming-design.md`. No product code.

DRAG-TO-ASK — UI HALF (item 3, part A). Let the founder drag a priority, ticket, or run from the dashboard into the Oz terminal so it attaches as a lightweight pointer (NOT the file body) and is included when they ask Oz about it. Keep this atom SELF-CONTAINED in the renderer: do NOT change the daemon, IPC contract, preload, or main process; thread the pointer through the EXISTING `onSend(text: string)` seam. Daemon-side path resolution + scoped answering is a SEPARATE later atom.

Files in scope: `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/Priorities.tsx`, `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx`, `packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/OzChat.tsx`, and the UI tests (`packages/ui/tests/ozchat.test.tsx` and, if you add drag tests for the lists, a sibling test file). Touch nothing else.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. DRAG SOURCES set a pointer payload. On dragStart for priority cards (Priorities.tsx), ticket cards (TicketsTab in Dashboard.tsx), and run rows (RunsTab in Dashboard.tsx — these are currently click-only; add `draggable`), call `e.dataTransfer.setData('application/x-oz-item', JSON.stringify({ itemType, id, label }))` where itemType is 'priority'|'ticket'|'run', id is the item's id, and label is its human name/title/displayName. Use a DISTINCT MIME type so this does NOT interfere with the existing list REORDER drag — reorder for priorities/tickets and click-to-select for runs MUST still work unchanged. Do not break the existing {from,over} reorder state.
2. DROP TARGET on the Oz composer. In OzChatPanel, add onDragOver (preventDefault so drop is allowed, only when the drag carries 'application/x-oz-item') and onDrop handlers on the input/composer area. On drop, parse the pointer JSON and store it in local component state (e.g. `attachedPointer`). Show a subtle drop-affordance (e.g. highlighted border) while a valid item is dragged over.
3. POINTER CHIP. When a pointer is attached, render a small removable chip directly above the textarea showing the itemType + label (and id), with an × button that clears `attachedPointer`. Only one pointer at a time is fine (a new drop replaces it).
4. INCLUDE ON SEND. In OzChatPanel's existing `send()`, if a pointer is attached, PREPEND a clear, machine-and-human readable reference line to the outgoing text before calling `onSend(...)`, e.g. `[context: <itemType> <id> — <label>]\n` followed by the typed text, then clear `attachedPointer` along with the text. This keeps the daemon unchanged: Oz receives the reference in the message and can resolve the item from its existing awareness. Sending with an attached pointer but EMPTY typed text should still be allowed (the founder may just drop an item and hit send to ask 'where does this stand?'); in that case send just the reference line.
5. TESTS (extend ozchat.test.tsx): dropping an 'application/x-oz-item' payload renders the chip with the label; the × removes it; clicking Send with a pointer attached calls onSend with text that contains the `[context: <itemType> <id>` reference; a pointer + typed text includes BOTH; and existing run-card, decision, and markdown tests still pass. If you add list-drag tests, prove dragStart sets the 'application/x-oz-item' data and that existing reorder behavior is untouched.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE: `pnpm --dir packages/ui test` passes (all suites, including the new drag cases) and `pnpm --dir packages/ui run typecheck` exits 0 (both tsconfig projects). Provide the exact commands and exit status. Do NOT modify daemon/IPC/preload/main; do NOT rework the Dashboard grid, workspace tabs, ResizeHandle, or panel ratio (archived workspace-segmentation seam).
